MW

Mei Wan

RD — Registered Dietitian & Nutritionist
Registered Dietitian & Nutritionist — Metabolic Health & Weight Loss

Mei Wan is a Registered Dietitian and Nutritionist working with Ted's Health to support the metabolic and nutritional dimensions of men's health. Her specialism is weight loss and type 2 diabetes remission — two areas that intersect closely with testosterone levels, where low T and metabolic dysfunction often reinforce each other in a difficult cycle.

Mei's role at Ted's Health reflects a broader clinical reality: TRT alone doesn't address the full picture for men with metabolic health challenges. Diet, body composition, and insulin sensitivity all interact with hormone levels — and optimising them can dramatically improve treatment outcomes.

Men with low testosterone are more likely to carry excess visceral fat, which itself suppresses testosterone production. Mei works with patients for whom lifestyle intervention is either a standalone strategy or a complement to TRT — helping them achieve results that medication alone can't deliver.

"Hormones and metabolism are deeply connected. For many of the men I see, the most powerful thing we can do isn't a prescription — it's a set of targeted dietary changes that work with the body's biology."

— Mei Wan, Registered Dietitian & Nutritionist, Ted's Health

Areas of expertise

⚖️
Weight Loss Evidence-based dietary interventions for sustainable weight loss in men.
🩸
Type 2 Diabetes Remission Dietary approaches to blood sugar regulation and, in many cases, full remission.
🧬
Metabolic Health & TRT Nutritional support for men on TRT — optimising body composition and treatment outcomes.
🥗
Personalised Nutrition Individual dietary plans tailored to health goals, food preferences, and clinical markers.
🎓
HCPC registeredRegistered Dietitian (RD)
🧬
Metabolic specialistWeight loss & type 2 diabetes
🤝
Integrated careWorks alongside TRT team
📊
Evidence-basedClinical nutrition protocols

Ted's Health is a CQC-registered provider of men's health treatments in the UK. All treatments are UK-licensed and prescribed by registered clinicians. This page is for informational purposes. For medical advice, please consult one of our doctors.
